Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for NorthWind deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind" or similar) or crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind"). Discovery: Multiple NorthWind deals exist (a) deal_northwind: "NorthWind Technologies 5 Enterprise Relocations" at $60,000, stage proposalMade; (b) deal_northwind_won: same deal at closedWon stage; (c) deal_northwind_denver_expansion: "NorthWind Technologies -- Denver Satellite Office Expansion (2 Relocations)" at $14,700, stage qualifiedToBuy, close date 2026-04-23, company_id company_northwind; (d) deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2: same Denver deal at contractSent stage with detailed descriptions of both Raj Patel (server lab, chain-of-custody, hazmat) and Emilia Cruz (Steinway piano, 27-day lease overlap) relocations. The agent discovers NorthWind is managed by Catalina Dubois, NorthWind's HR contact is Pam Kowalski (pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com), and the CISO is Victor Huang (victor.huang@northwindtech.com).
OE 2: Search CRM engagements for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) or by finding company_northwind first via crm_search_companies. Discovery: The engagement eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context contains the full account recovery context NorthWind signed a $60,000 contract in January 2026 for 5 relocations at $12K each, execution was poor (two Phase 1 apartments failed quality standards HVAC malfunction in Austin, misrepresented listing in Phoenix), Catalina's response time to Pam Kowalski's complaint was unacceptably slow, Pam escalated directly to David Chen, NorthWind's Q1 satisfaction survey was 5/10 overall, 4/10 on communication, 5/10 on service quality. Catalina committed to enhanced SLA including dedicated coordinator, daily status updates, and David cc'd on all NorthWind communications. This engagement reveals the commitments Catalina made.
OE 3: Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: "NorthWind Technologies") or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations). Discovery: Multiple NorthWind relocation records found (a) Completed Phase 1 relocations: Ryan Caldwell (Detroit→Chicago, Feb 17-18, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi), Lisa Petrosky (Indianapolis→Chicago, Feb 19-20, coordinator Javier Morales), Derek Nash (Minneapolis→Chicago, Feb 24-25, coordinator Blessing Okafor); (b) Completed Phase 2: Amira Okonkwo (St. Louis→Chicago, Mar 7-8, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi), Ben Schaefer (Milwaukee→Chicago, Mar 10-11, coordinator Javier Morales); (c) Denver expansion In Progress: Raj Patel (Chicago→Denver, Apr 21-23, coordinator Blessing Okafor, server lab with chain-of-custody requirements), Emilia Cruz (Chicago→Denver, Apr 14-18, coordinator Blessing Okafor, Steinway piano); (d) Audit note record recRajPatelAuditNote0411 showing that the escort was confirmed for 9:00 AM but movers arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting.
OE 4: Search emails for the Raj Patel security concern using search_emails (query: "Raj Patel" or "Victor Huang" or "chain of custody" or "security incident" or similar). Discovery: (a) Victor Huang's original security requirements email to Catalina 5 non-negotiable requirements for the server lab move: (1) data preparation and encryption verification, (2) supervised packing and loading with NorthWind IT security escort, (3) direct transport with no interim warehouse storage, (4) signed chain-of-custody log, (5) real-time GPS tracking; (b) Victor Huang's breach escalation email to Chloe (cc: Catalina) crew arrived and loaded before the 9:00 AM IT security escort, equipment was placed into overnight warehouse storage violating the no-interim-storage requirement, Victor is treating this as a formal security incident. The agent discovers the two key failures from Victor's breach email: unsupervised loading and unauthorized overnight warehouse storage. (Note: Chloe Vance's detailed ops timeline reconstruction confirming crew arrival at 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, load proceeded before escort, overnight storage at SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse is found via Slack in OE 9 and Linear comments in OE 8, not in emails.)
OE 5: Search emails for the Emilia Cruz Steinway damage using search_emails (query: "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" or "piano damage" or "KeyMove" or similar). Discovery: (a) Craig Nguyen (KeyMove Specialty Transport) sent damage photos and extraction notes to Blessing surface scratch approximately 4.5-5 inches on bass side rim near keyboard cheek block, occurred during extraction at second-floor landing when piano was rotated more aggressively than planned; (b) Catalina's April 14 email to Pam acknowledging both the Raj Patel handling issue and the Emilia Cruz piano damage, promising a comprehensive service recovery plan within the week; (c) Marcus Thorne's email to David (cc: Catalina, Chloe) noting KeyMove added a $1,200 insurance claim rider for the piano scratch. (Note: Julian Brooks' support call note documenting that Emilia called on April 12 requesting a same-day callback about whether MoveOps would take responsibility callback did not happen is found in CRM engagement crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c and in Slack #customer-support via OE 9, not in emails. The engagement crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c has empty company_ids and contact_ids, so it is not returned by the company-filtered crm_list_engagements call in OE 2; the agent would need to discover it via a broader engagement search, or via Slack #customer-support in OE 9, or by searching engagements without a company filter.)
OE 6: Search emails for Pam Kowalski's escalation email to David using search_emails (query: "Pam Kowalski" or "NorthWind retention" or "NorthWind evaluating" or similar). Discovery: Pam Kowalski sent a formal final-warning escalation email to David (cc: Victor Huang) stating that NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue to support the account. She cited two incidents: (1) Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach equipment loaded before escort, overnight warehouse storage; (2) Emilia Cruz piano damage and lack of timely follow-up. She states Catalina's April 14 recovery plan commitment was never delivered and mentions PathFinder as an alternative vendor. She requests a concrete proposal by end of day Friday, covering corrective actions for Raj Patel, resolution and reimbursement for Emilia Cruz, named account coverage with response-time expectations, and any commercial consideration.
OE 7: Search emails for David Chen's "retention package needed today" email using search_emails (query: "retention package" or "2:00 PM" or similar). Discovery: David's email (sent to Chloe, Julian, Marcus, Alejandro, Catalina, cc: Elena) asks each person for specific inputs by 2:00 PM Pacific. Catalina's specific ask is: "complete list of outstanding commitments to NorthWind, including anything promised verbally or by email that has not yet been delivered." This is the email the prompt directly references it sets the task and deadline.
OE 8: Search Linear for the NorthWind retention ticket David created using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or similar), then retrieve its comments using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1"). Discovery: Issue #482 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (id: linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1), priority 1, due 2026-04-24, assigned to David Chen (moveops_david_chen), team_operations. Description references: Victor Huang security breach, Chloe's ops timeline, missing custody-log documentation gap, Julian's note about Emilia Cruz same-day callback miss, Catalina's April 14 commitment that was not delivered, stale CRM trail, and Alejandro's draft retention model. The agent also finds associated comments including Chloe's reconstructed ops timeline (linear_comment_fc9fd7990fec), Alejandro's draft retention concession note (linear_comment_b38c11307d3a), and David's cross-system analysis noting the CRM does not reflect the true account severity (linear_comment_dd1f4bceee90). The issue description also references a missing custody-log / documentation integrity gap; the detailed comment on that problem (linear_comment_093d903c5cb9) lives on a separate issue (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind), not on #482 itself an agent following the reference in #482's description or in Chloe's Slack posts would discover it there.
OE 9: Search Slack for internal discussions about both moves using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" or "chain of custody" or "Steinway" or similar). Discovery: (a) Catalina's #customer-engagement post announcing the Denver expansion and Q1 history (5/10 satisfaction, Pam's escalation); (b) David's response in #customer-engagement approving enhanced SLA with dedicated coordinator, daily status updates, David cc'd on everything; (c) Blessing's #operations post owning the Emilia Cruz piano damage she green-lit the extraction plan off a thin assessment; (d) Julian's #customer-support post about Emilia Cruz calling in upset about the Steinway scratch, requesting same-day callback; (e) Catalina's #operations post about being overwhelmed with NorthWind's six active relocations; (f) Marcus's #finance post about the $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider and NorthWind becoming "expensive and fragile"; (g) David's #customer-engagement request for clean NorthWind status from Catalina by noon; (h) David's #executive post requesting permission to redistribute Catalina's accounts with Elena's response to handle it within the team; (i) Hana Kim's #finance post flagging the provisional chain-of-custody line item ($1,200) on invoice INV-2026-0412, questioning the early lease termination fee ($500), and noting that hazmat and piano lines are preliminary she questions the per-move pricing ($7,350/move) relative to NorthWind's original $12K/move Q1 rate and asks Marcus how to handle the provisional items before sending to Catalina.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks for the NorthWind invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to "NorthWind" or similar). Discovery: Invoice INV-2026-0412 (id: inv_northwind_denver_02) for NorthWind Technologies, total $14,700, with 6 line items: (1) Standard Relocation Package for Raj Patel: $4,500; (2) Hazmat Handling Surcharge for lithium UPS batteries: $1,800; (3) Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol: $1,200 marked PROVISIONAL, first-time service for MoveOps, actual cost pending vendor confirmation from Swift, may require invoice revision; (4) Standard Relocation Package for Emilia Cruz: $4,500; (5) Specialty Piano Moving (Steinway Model B, climate-controlled, tuning): $2,200 marked PROVISIONAL, pending subcontractor vendor agreement and insurance verification; (6) Early Lease Termination Negotiation Service: $500. Two line items are explicitly provisional and Pam has not signed off on them. There is also a separate $1,200 KeyMove bill (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417) for the piano damage insurance claim rider. The older invoice INV-2026-003 for the original 5 Enterprise Relocations at $60,000 also exists.
OE 11: Look up David Chen's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen" or "David") or contacts_get_contacts. Discovery: david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 12: Update the NorthWind CRM engagement trail using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or similar, body: account status update reflecting the current severity Pam Kowalski has escalated past Catalina directly to David, NorthWind is evaluating whether to continue with MoveOps, PathFinder mentioned as alternative vendor, Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach unresolved, Emilia Cruz piano damage claim in process with $1,200 KeyMove insurance rider, Catalina's April 14 recovery plan commitment was not delivered, two provisional invoice line items ($1,200 chain-of-custody, $2,200 specialty piano) still unsigned, Linear retention ticket #482 open with April 24 deadline). The engagement should reference company_ids including company_northwind (or contact_ids including contact_pam_kowalski or contact_victor_huang). This updates the CRM so it no longer reads like "business as usual."
OE 13: Send email to David Chen (via send_email) from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com with the complete list of commitments made to Pam and Victor, delivery status of each, and recommendations for unresolved items. The email content must cover:
Commitments made and their status:
(a) Comprehensive service recovery plan promised to Pam on April 14 NOT delivered (Catalina's April 14 email committed to a plan "within the week" covering the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown, Emilia Cruz damage resolution, and service changes; this was never sent)
(b) Enhanced SLA with dedicated coordinator and daily status updates committed in #customer-engagement by David, Catalina acknowledged. Partially implemented (Blessing assigned as coordinator) but daily status emails were not consistently delivered during the crisis period
(c) David cc'd on all NorthWind communications committed by David in #customer-engagement. Implemented on some but not all communications
(d) Raj Patel chain-of-custody protocol committed to NorthWind via Victor Huang's security requirements document (5 non-negotiable controls: encryption verification, supervised loading, direct transport, chain-of-custody log, GPS tracking). BREACHED crew loaded at 7:00 AM before 9:00 AM escort, equipment placed in overnight warehouse storage
(e) Emilia Cruz same-day callback after April 12 support call Julian told Emilia someone would call back the same day. NOT delivered (no owner picked it up)
(f) Emilia Cruz piano damage resolution open, KeyMove submitted $1,200 insurance claim rider, no formal resolution communicated to Emilia or Pam
(g) Provisional invoice items ($1,200 chain-of-custody, $2,200 specialty piano moving) on INV-2026-0412 not yet confirmed with Pam, pending vendor confirmation
Recommendations for each unresolved item:
- For the undelivered recovery plan: propose sending it immediately as part of David's retention response
- For the chain-of-custody breach: recommend a formal written acknowledgment to Victor Huang with corrective measures, referencing the new hazmat documentation policy Chloe is implementing
- For the Emilia Cruz callback and damage: recommend a direct outreach to Emilia with a clear resolution path on the damage claim, and ensure Pam receives a concurrent update
- For provisional invoice items: recommend finalizing pricing with Swift for chain-of-custody and with the piano subcontractor before presenting to Pam, or converting to actual costs
- For CRM update: confirm CRM has been updated to reflect actual account severity
OE 14: Post a message in the #customer-engagement Slack channel (via conversations_add_message, channel_id: C002) confirming that Catalina's inputs for the NorthWind retention response are done and have been sent to David. The message should confirm the list of commitments and open items has been delivered so the rest of the team knows her piece is complete.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

The issue description also references a missing custody-log / documentation integrity gap; the detailed comment on that problem (linear_comment_093d903c5cb9) lives on a separate issue (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind), not on #482 itself an agent following the reference in #482's description or in Chloe's Slack posts would discover it there.
OE 9: Search Slack for internal discussions about both moves using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" or "chain of custody" or "Steinway" or similar). Discovery: (a) Catalina's #customer-engagement post announcing the Denver expansion and Q1 history (5/10 satisfaction, Pam's escalation); (b) David's response in #customer-engagement approving enhanced SLA with dedicated coordinator, daily status updates, David cc'd on everything; (c) Blessing's #operations post owning the Emilia Cruz piano damage she green-lit the extraction plan off a thin assessment; (d) Julian's #customer-support post about Emilia Cruz calling in upset about the Steinway scratch, requesting same-day callback; (e) Catalina's #operations post about being overwhelmed with NorthWind's six active relocations; (f) Marcus's #finance post about the $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider and NorthWind becoming "expensive and fragile"; (g) David's #customer-engagement request for clean NorthWind status from Catalina by noon; (h) David's #executive post requesting permission to redistribute Catalina's accounts with Elena's response to handle it within the team; (i) Hana Kim's #finance post flagging the provisional chain-of-custody line item ($1,200) on invoice INV-2026-0412, questioning the early lease termination fee ($500), and noting that hazmat and piano lines are preliminary she questions the per-move pricing ($7,350/move) relative to NorthWind's original $12K/move Q1 rate and asks Marcus how to handle the provisional items before sending to Catalina.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks for the NorthWind invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to "NorthWind" or similar). Discovery: Invoice INV-2026-0412 (id: inv_northwind_denver_02) for NorthWind Technologies, total $14,700, with 6 line items: (1) Standard Relocation Package for Raj Patel: $4,500; (2) Hazmat Handling Surcharge for lithium UPS batteries: $1,800; (3) Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol: $1,200 marked PROVISIONAL, first-time service for MoveOps, actual cost pending vendor confirmation from Swift, may require invoice revision; (4) Standard Relocation Package for Emilia Cruz: $4,500; (5) Specialty Piano Moving (Steinway Model B, climate-controlled, tuning): $2,200 marked PROVISIONAL, pending subcontractor vendor agreement and insurance verification; (6) Early Lease Termination Negotiation Service: $500. Two line items are explicitly provisional and Pam has not signed off on them. There is also a separate $1,200 KeyMove bill (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417) for the piano damage insurance claim rider. The older invoice INV-2026-003 for the original 5 Enterprise Relocations at $60,000 also exists.
OE 11: Look up David Chen's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen" or "David") or contacts_get_contacts. Discovery: david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 12: Update the NorthWind CRM engagement trail using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or similar, body: account status update reflecting the current severity Pam Kowalski has escalated past Catalina directly to David, NorthWind is evaluating whether to continue with MoveOps, PathFinder mentioned as alternative vendor, Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach unresolved, Emilia Cruz piano damage claim in process with $1,200 KeyMove insurance rider, Catalina's April 14 recovery plan commitment was not delivered, two provisional invoice line items ($1,200 chain-of-custody, $2,200 specialty piano) still unsigned, Linear retention ticket #482 open with April 24 deadline). The engagement should reference company_ids including company_northwind (or contact_ids including contact_pam_kowalski or contact_victor_huang). This updates the CRM so it no longer reads like "business as usual."
OE 13: Send email to David Chen (via send_email) from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com with the complete list of commitments made to Pam and Victor, delivery status of each, and recommendations for unresolved items. The email content must cover:
Commitments made and their status:
(a) Comprehensive service recovery plan promised to Pam on April 14 NOT delivered (Catalina's April 14 email committed to a plan "within the week" covering the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown, Emilia Cruz damage resolution, and service changes; this was never sent)
(b) Enhanced SLA with dedicated coordinator and daily status updates committed in #customer-engagement by David, Catalina acknowledged. Partially implemented (Blessing assigned as coordinator) but daily status emails were not consistently delivered during the crisis period
(c) David cc'd on all NorthWind communications committed by David in #customer-engagement. Implemented on some but not all communications
(d) Raj Patel chain-of-custody protocol committed to NorthWind via Victor Huang's security requirements document (5 non-negotiable controls: encryption verification, supervised loading, direct transport, chain-of-custody log, GPS tracking). BREACHED crew loaded at 7:00 AM before 9:00 AM escort, equipment placed in overnight warehouse storage
(e) Emilia Cruz same-day callback after April 12 support call Julian told Emilia someone would call back the same day. NOT delivered (no owner picked it up)
(f) Emilia Cruz piano damage resolution open, KeyMove submitted $1,200 insurance claim rider, no formal resolution communicated to Emilia or Pam
(g) Provisional invoice items ($1,200 chain-of-custody, $2,200 specialty piano moving) on INV-2026-0412 not yet confirmed with Pam, pending vendor confirmation
Recommendations for each unresolved item:
- For the undelivered recovery plan: propose sending it immediately as part of David's retention response
- For the chain-of-custody breach: recommend a formal written acknowledgment to Victor Huang with corrective measures, referencing the new hazmat documentation policy Chloe is implementing
- For the Emilia Cruz callback and damage: recommend a direct outreach to Emilia with a clear resolution path on the damage claim, and ensure Pam receives a concurrent update
- For provisional invoice items: recommend finalizing pricing with Swift for chain-of-custody and with the piano subcontractor before presenting to Pam, or converting to actual costs
- For CRM update: confirm CRM has been updated to reflect actual account severity
OE 14: Post a message in the #customer-engagement Slack channel (via conversations_add_message, channel_id: C002) confirming that Catalina's inputs for the NorthWind retention response are done and have been sent to David. The message should confirm the list of commitments and open items has been delivered so the rest of the team knows her piece is complete.
